meta.franchisorLegalName: MTY Franchising USA, Inc. dba Grabbagreen

item1.franchisorLegalName: MTY Franchising USA, Inc. dba Grabbagreen
item1.principalAddress: 9311 E Via De Ventura, Scottsdale, AZ 85258

predecessors.name: The Extreme Pita Franchising USA, Inc.

affiliates.name: Kahala Management, L.L.C.
affiliates.services: admin, legal, accounting, sales, POS support, real estate, marketing

item1.businessDescription: Quick service restaurants selling healthy food, juice, smoothies, and related products

item3.litigationNote: Substantial legacy and affiliate litigation history. Cases are affiliate-level (Kahala Franchising), not brand-level operating metrics.

item6.dualFormulaNote: Greater of 6% of weekly gross sales OR $400/week for traditional units
item6.advertisingFundNote: No obligation to spend in franchisee market area. Held in general accounts.
item6.breachingRoyalty: Greater of 3x fixed royalty or up to 18% of gross sales for at least 14 days

otherFees.name: Management fee (if franchisor assumes)
otherFees.amount: 6% of gross sales plus direct costs

item7.investmentNote: Does not include real estate costs and/or rent except initial security deposit

item8.franchisorIsDirectSupplierNote: MTY USA says it is not a supplier

affiliateSuppliers.name: Neptune Equipment Services, LLC
affiliateSuppliers.products: equipment, menu boards, furniture, wall graphics, smallwares

affiliateSuppliers.name: Kahala Management, L.L.C.
affiliateSuppliers.services: POS help-desk phone support (sole approved provider)

item8.operationalVendorNote: Exterminators, refrigeration service, refuse removal can be chosen freely

item12.protectedAreaNote: Franchise agreement says non-exclusive, no territorial protection. But Item 12 uses 'Protected Territory' language and gives ROFR. Franchisor reserves all rights including other concepts, other channels, selling to customers anywhere including protected territory.
item12.franchisorCompeteDetails: May place other Grabbagreen units, other concepts, other channels, sell goods/services anywhere including protected territory
item12.onlineSalesDetails: Franchisee may not sell through internet or alternative channels except through approved franchise system website
item12.encroachmentNote: Despite 'protected territory' language, franchisor retains broad reserved rights making protection largely nominal

term.termNote: 10 years if franchisee owns property or leases directly. Sublease term if subleasing from MTY/affiliate.

renewal.renewalNote: Renewal agreement can contain materially different terms including higher royalty and advertising fees

nonCompete.postTermNote: No competing business for 2 years within 10 miles of another Grabbagreen restaurant

item17.spousalGuarantyNote: Spouse must sign Guaranty of Franchise Agreement and Non-Disclosure/Non-Competition Agreement

item19.fprNote: Franchisor does not make representations about future financial performance or past performance of company-owned or franchised outlets

item20.systemNote: Only 4 open franchised units. Zero projected openings. Non-expanding system.
item20.confidentialityWarning: Some current/former franchisees may have signed clauses restricting open discussion of experience

item21.materialLeaseNote: Material operating lease liabilities and expected sublease income. System uses affiliate-held master leases and subleases.

maryland.note: All initial fees and payments deferred until franchisor completes pre-opening obligations

universalRules: fprAvailable=false must hard-stop any revenue/profit fill from Item 19
Territory needs richer state than boolean: nonexclusive_with_protected_area_and_reserved_rights
Royalty must support dual formula: % of sales plus minimum weekly floor
Fee model must support state surcharge, breaching royalty, annual meeting debit, data polling, document admin, relocation, renewal, transfer fees
Supplier model must split franchisor-not-supplier from affiliate-suppliers
State addenda must be first-class because Maryland materially changes payment timing
Item 20 should capture projected_openings=zero which is buyer-relevant
Guarantee logic must capture spousal signature and entity-owner guaranties
